threats.title: MD5 Password Hashing — Trivial Offline Cracking
threats.scenario: models/user.ts:77 calls security.hash(clearTextPassword) which uses crypto.createHash('md5'). After SQLi dump (T-003/T-004), attacker cracks all hashes with rainbow tables within hours. TOTP secrets stored plaintext.
threats.controls_in_place: None — MD5 is not a KDF

steps: Replace MD5 with bcrypt (rounds=12) or Argon2id
Force password reset for all users after migration

threats.title: SQLite Database File Unencrypted at Rest
threats.scenario: data/juiceshop.sqlite stored unencrypted on Docker volume. If container filesystem is accessible (misconfigured volume mount, container escape), attacker reads all user data, passwords, payment cards, and TOTP secrets.

steps: Enable SQLCipher or full-disk encryption for the Docker volume
Alternatively migrate to PostgreSQL with pgcrypto for field-level encryption

threats.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference — Basket/Order Ownership Bypass
threats.scenario: Basket and order routes accept numeric IDs from authenticated users without ownership checks. Sequential SQLite autoincrement IDs are predictable. Authenticated user can view/modify other users' baskets.
threats.controls_in_place: Authentication required; auth token tied to user

steps: Add UserId ownership check: basket.UserId !== currentUser.data.id → 403
Use opaque non-sequential IDs (UUID) for resource identifiers
